paper_title: Location, Field, and Laboratory Methods for Foraminiferal Sampling

methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting foraminiferal samples from specific locations in Jamaica to study environmental variables and foraminiferal diversity.

steps.description: Four replicate foraminiferal samples were taken each month by inserting core liners (internal diameter 2.54 cm) into randomly chosen cells.
steps.searchable_summary: collect foraminiferal samples using core liners

steps.description: Buffered formalin was added in the field, and upon returning to the laboratory the top 20 ml were washed over a 63 µm sieve and stored in alcohol.
steps.searchable_summary: preserve and process samples with formalin and alcohol

methods.description: This method outlines the laboratory processing of foraminiferal samples to prepare them for examination and identification.

steps.description: The samples were transported to the foraminiferal laboratory at the National Museum of Natural History for further processing.
steps.searchable_summary: transport samples to laboratory for processing

steps.description: The day before examination 0.1 g of rose bengal was added to the sample. After shaking several times, the sample was washed over a 63 µm sieve, dried and floated twice in a mixture of bromoform and acetone having a specific gravity of 2.4.
steps.searchable_summary: stain and separate samples using rose bengal and bromoform-acetone mixture

steps.description: The floated portion of the sample was doled out into about 15 gridded petri dishes and rewet.
steps.searchable_summary: distribute and rewet samples in petri dishes

steps.description: All foraminifera were observed under transmitted and reflected light, and those showing stained protoplasm were picked wet and placed on slides for sorting and identification.
steps.searchable_summary: observe and sort foraminifera under light for identification

methods.description: This method involves measuring various environmental variables at the sampling stations to understand the conditions affecting foraminiferal populations.

steps.description: At the time of the field collections, 12 environmental variables were measured at each of the stations.
steps.searchable_summary: measure environmental variables at sampling stations
